sv.note: 60-65°C bath core for juicy ground sausage; 65°C for 1-2h fully pasteurizes ground meat (well above 54.4°C danger-zone limit, hold <4h)

smoke.note: ground meat MUST reach 160°F/71°C 'for safety, but no higher for juiciness'; raised tgt 70→71 to match ground floor; hot finish 90°C ~45min

smoke_only.note: hot-smoke ~225°F/107°C to internal 160°F/71°C (~1h at 225°F); 100°C/2.5h low-and-slow variant reaches 71°C safely; NEVER cold-smoke raw sausage

safe.note: GROUND meat floor 71°C/160°F — never lower; SV path pasteurizes at 65°C via hold but displayed floor stays 71 for grill/smoke-only no-hold path

grill.note: two-zone: cook on indirect (~400°F/200°C dome) to ~71°C core, then quick char on direct high heat; pull at 71°C (ground floor)

rationale: Kept svt 65 (pasteurizes ground via hold, Baldwin) and safe 71 (ground floor, AmazingRibs). Raised smoke tgt 70→71 so the final internal target meets the mandatory ground-meat floor — a ground final target below 71 is unsafe. Never cold-smoke raw sausage.

sv.ref: Baldwin — beef core temps (medium-rare 54.5°C); anticuchos tradition
sv.note: heart is lean muscle, best rare–medium-rare; 55°C ≈ medium-rare core, 2h tenderizes without graining

smoke.note: brief hot smoke for flavor/crust; core already 55°C, oak (אלון) is mild

smoke_only.note: heart cooks fast; short high-heat smoke/char to medium-rare, do not overcook or it turns rubbery

safe.note: heart is whole muscle → displayed floor 63°C; interior sterile so med-rare tgt 55 with seared surface is correct

grill.ref: Hank Shaw — Anticuchos Peruanos (grilled beef heart skewers)
grill.note: direct high heat ~500°F/260°C, 3-5 min turning / ~60-90s per side on skewers, pull medium-rare ~55°C; charred outside, pink inside

rationale: All values well-supported; kept svt 55 (med-rare muscle per Baldwin) and safe 63 (whole-muscle policy floor). Anticuchos tradition confirms hot-fast direct grilling to medium-rare.

sv.note: poultry offal; 68°C bath with ~1.5h hold pasteurizes (Baldwin poultry table: 65°C/20mm ≈ 40min), tender not chalky

smoke.note: quick hot smoke; apple (תפוח) mild for delicate poultry

smoke_only.ref: Healthy Recipes / grilling guides — hearts cook fast
smoke_only.note: small pieces; no-hold path must reach poultry floor 74°C

safe.note: poultry floor 74°C displayed; SV path legitimately pasteurizes at 68°C with hold (Baldwin) but grill/smoke-only no-hold must hit 74°C

grill.ref: Healthy Recipes Blog / grilling guides — chicken hearts
grill.note: skewer, direct high heat ~4-5 min turning; POULTRY no-hold → pull at 74°C not 68

rationale: Kept svt 68 (SV pasteurizes via hold, Baldwin poultry table) and safe 74 (poultry floor never lowered). Noted grill/smoke-only no-hold path must reach 74°C.

sv.note: chicken liver carries salmonella/campylobacter risk → cook to 74°C/165°F throughout; classic pâté doneness

smoke.note: delicate; brief mild apple smoke, do not dry out

smoke_only.note: must reach 74°C; liver overcooks quickly so keep short

safe.note: poultry liver floor 74°C; no SV lowering — pathogen risk is high in chicken liver

grill.note: skewer, direct medium-high, quick; pull at 74°C (poultry, no hold) — just cooked through, not dry

rationale: All values correct; svt=tgt=safe=74 for chicken liver is mandatory (poultry salmonella/campylobacter, USDA 165°F). Nothing lowered.

sv.ref: USDA organ meat 160°F; Serious Eats/offal doneness
sv.note: beef liver best cooked to medium/just-pink; 72°C keeps it above 71°C organ floor while avoiding grainy overcook

smoke.note: brief oak smoke for flavor; liver dries fast, keep short

smoke_only.note: reach 71-72°C then pull; overcooked liver turns chalky

safe.note: organ-meat floor 71°C; current 72 is conservative (≥floor) — kept

grill.note: thin slices/cubes, direct high heat ~1 min per side to ~72°C; do not overcook

rationale: Kept svt/tgt/safe 72 — matches USDA organ-meat floor (71°C) with a conservative round-up and beef-liver medium doneness. Documented existing values with citations.

sv.note: lamb liver same as beef liver — cook to just-set/medium, 72°C above organ floor without graining

smoke.note: cherry (דובדבן) mild-sweet, brief smoke; keep short

grill.ref: Grilling guides — liver kebab (e.g. Turkish ciğer)
grill.note: cubes/thin slices, direct high heat, seconds per side to ~72°C

rationale: Kept 72 across svt/tgt/safe — matches USDA organ floor (71°C) and lamb-liver medium doneness. Documented, no change.

sv.ref: ChefSteps — Foie Gras Torchon; Anova terrine
sv.note: foie is served gentle/mi-cuit; torchon methods run 55-60°C, a firmer cooked terrine ~65°C — kept 65 for a set terrine while minimizing fat render

smoke.note: very brief/low; wood ללא (none) — foie is delicate, heavy smoke overwhelms and renders fat

smoke_only.note: keep low to avoid melting away the fat; pull ~65°C

safe.note: foie gras is traditionally served mi-cuit BELOW the 71°C organ floor; documented as a gentle-temp delicacy (like rare foie), floor kept at its served temp 65°C

grill.ref: Culinary practice — foie is pan-seared, not grilled
grill.note: NOT grilled — rendering fat flares on open grate; slices are flash pan-seared 30-45s per side in a screaming-hot dry pan

rationale: Kept svt/tgt/safe 65 — foie gras is served mi-cuit at a gentle temp (ChefSteps torchon 55-60°C, cooked terrine ~65°C), deliberately below the 71°C organ floor as a delicacy. Marked not grillable (pan-seared instead).

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy / Anova — sweetbreads
sv.note: sweetbreads are soaked/blanched then SV; medium ~60°C, 65°C for a firmer set that presses well; then floured & seared golden ~2 min/side

smoke.note: brief oak smoke after poach/press; then crisp sear

smoke_only.note: poach/press first; brief smoke to warm through ~65°C

safe.ref: Poached/seared offal — cooked through at serving temp
safe.note: sweetbreads (thymus/pancreas) are blanched then cooked through and seared; 65°C set-and-served floor documented

grill.note: must be soaked/blanched/pressed first; then grill direct medium-high to crisp exterior, ~2 min/side (Argentine mollejas de asador)

rationale: Kept svt/tgt/safe 65 — sweetbreads are blanched then poached/SV to a firm set (~60-65°C per Amazing Food Made Easy) and seared; grillable after pressing (mollejas). No change.

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy / Great British Chefs — sweetbreads
sv.note: same as veal sweetbreads — soak/blanch then SV ~60-65°C set, then flour & sear golden

smoke.note: brief cherry smoke after poach; crisp sear

safe.ref: Poached/seared offal — cooked through at serving temp
safe.note: blanched then cooked through and seared; 65°C set-and-served floor documented

grill.note: soak/blanch/press first, then grill direct medium-high ~2 min/side to crisp

rationale: Kept svt/tgt/safe 65 — lamb sweetbreads follow the same blanch/poach-to-set (~60-65°C) then sear method. Grillable after pressing. No change.

sv.ref: Forager Chef — gizzard/heart confit; collagen breakdown
sv.note: gizzards are tough collagen — need long low cook (confit ~250°F/121°C ~3h, or SV 90°C ~4h) to turn tender; texture target, not doneness target

smoke.note: long enough to reach tender ~90°C internal; apple smoke

smoke_only.note: low-and-slow to ~90°C tender; short cooks leave gizzards rubbery

safe.note: poultry floor 74°C; tgt 90 far exceeds it (texture-driven), floor stays 74 — never lowered

grill.note: tough collagen — MUST be confited/braised tender first, then quick direct char; do NOT direct-grill raw (rubbery)

rationale: Kept svt/tgt 90 (collagen tenderness target, confit/long-SV per Forager Chef) and safe 74 (poultry floor). Grill only after pre-cooking tender. No change.

sv.ref: USDA organ meat 160°F; Anova beef kidney
sv.note: kidney cooked medium/just-pink then finished; 72°C keeps it above 71°C organ floor while staying tender (overcooked kidney turns rubbery)

smoke.note: brief oak smoke; kidney firms fast, keep short

smoke_only.note: reach ~72°C, pull while plump/firm not rubbery

grill.note: halve/butterfly, direct high heat ~1-2 min/side to just firm; do not overcook

rationale: Kept 72 across svt/tgt/safe — matches USDA organ floor (71°C) with conservative round-up; kidney cooked just-firm. Documented, no change.

sv.ref: USDA organ meat 160°F; grilled lamb kidney doneness
sv.note: lamb kidney classically grilled just-pink; 72°C above organ floor while staying plump/tender

smoke.note: brief cherry smoke; keep short so kidney stays tender

grill.ref: Food52 — Grilled Lamb Kidneys with Crispy Sage
grill.note: halve, direct high heat ~1-2 min/side, charred outside just-firm inside; overcooking makes them rubbery

rationale: Kept 72 across svt/tgt/safe — matches USDA organ floor (71°C). Grilling confirmed direct high heat ~1-2 min/side (Food52). No change.

sv.ref: Offally Good Cooking — poaching brains; Julia Child
sv.note: brains are poached just below a simmer (~20 min for calf) to a custardy set, then floured & pan-fried meunière; 65°C SV core mirrors that gentle set

smoke.note: very gentle/brief; wood ללא (none) — brain is fragile and mild, heavy smoke ruins it

smoke_only.note: keep low; custardy texture, err toward gentle

safe.ref: Poached delicacy — cooked custardy at serving temp
safe.note: brains poached just below simmer to custardy set (~65°C); documented served floor, kept — a fragile poached offal, not a whole-muscle roast

grill.ref: Culinary practice — cervelle is poached then pan-fried, not grilled
grill.note: NOT grillable — too soft/custardy for grates; classic cervelle de veau meunière is poached then floured and pan-seared in brown butter 3-4 min/side

rationale: Kept svt/tgt/safe 65 — brains are poached just below a simmer to a custardy set (Offally Good Cooking / Julia Child) then pan-fried meunière. Marked not grillable (too fragile for grates).
